ddls:
CREATE TABLE "athletes" (
  "athlete_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each athlete */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the athlete */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the athlete */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Date of birth of the athlete */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the athlete */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the athlete */,
  "sport_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sport the athlete participates in */,
  "height" REAL /* Height of the athlete in meters */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight of the athlete in kilograms */,
  "injured" INTEGER /* Indicates if the athlete is currently injured */,
  "injury_details" TEXT /* Details of the current injury, if any */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("athlete_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "competitions" (
  "competition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each competition */,
  "competition_name" TEXT /* Name of the competition */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the competition */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the competition */,
  "sport_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sport the competition is for */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the competition */,
  "level" TEXT /* Level of the competition */,
  "organizer" TEXT /* Organization hosting the competition */,
  "number_of_participants" INTEGER /* Number of participants in the competition */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("competition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each result entry */,
  "competition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the competition */,
  "athlete_id" INTEGER /* ID of the athlete */,
  "position" INTEGER /* Position the athlete finished in the competition */,
  "score" REAL /* Score achieved by the athlete */,
  "sport_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sport the result is for */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the specific event within the competition */,
  "time" REAL /* Time taken by the athlete (if applicable) */,
  "distance" REAL /* Distance covered by the athlete (if applicable) */,
  "points" INTEGER /* Points awarded to the athlete */,
  "medal" TEXT /* Medal won by the athlete */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_results_athlete_id FOREIGN KEY ("athlete_id") REFERENCES athletes ("athlete_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_results_competition_id FOREIGN KEY ("competition_id") REFERENCES competitions ("competition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "training_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training session */,
  "athlete_id" INTEGER /* ID of the athlete conducting the session */,
  "session_date" TEXT /* Date of the training session */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the training session in hours */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of training conducted */,
  "sport_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sport the training session is for */,
  "coach_id" INTEGER /* ID of the coach conducting the session */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the training session took place */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions during the session */,
  "equipment_used" TEXT /* List of equipment used during the session */,
  "session_effectiveness" REAL /* Score indicating the effectiveness of the session */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_training_sessions_coach_id FOREIGN KEY ("coach_id") REFERENCES coaches ("coach_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_training_sessions_athlete_id FOREIGN KEY ("athlete_id") REFERENCES athletes ("athlete_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "coaches" (
  "coach_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each coach */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the coach */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the coach */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the coach */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the coach */,
  "sport_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sport the coach specializes in */,
  "certifications" TEXT /* List of certifications the coach holds */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Number of years the coach has been coaching */,
  "specialty" TEXT /* Coach's specialty */,
  "availability" TEXT /* Coach's availability schedule */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("coach_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "athlete_coach_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship entry */,
  "athlete_id" INTEGER /* ID of the athlete */,
  "coach_id" INTEGER /* ID of the coach */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the relationship started */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the relationship ended (if applicable) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the relationship */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_athlete_coach_relationships_athlete_id FOREIGN KEY ("athlete_id") REFERENCES athletes ("athlete_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_athlete_coach_relationships_coach_id FOREIGN KEY ("coach_id") REFERENCES coaches ("coach_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rankings" (
  "ranking_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ranking entry */,
  "athlete_id" INTEGER /* ID of the athlete */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the ranking */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the athlete for that year */,
  "sport_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sport the ranking is for */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the ranking */,
  "level" TEXT /* Level of the ranking */,
  "points" INTEGER /* Points contributing to the ranking */,
  "number_of_competitions" INTEGER /* Number of competitions participated in to achieve the ranking */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ranking_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rankings_athlete_id FOREIGN KEY ("athlete_id") REFERENCES athletes ("athlete_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_accounts" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user account */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the account */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the account */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_accounts_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)
